Staff Engineer • Engineering Manager • Backend • Platform

I build backend and platform systems that scale, and I care about creating the team systems that help engineers scale too.

My work sits across backend, platform, and distributed systems, especially where architecture, performance, modernization, and team execution all need to move together.

~30s -> <1s~1,000 req/sec$3M -> $15M MRR

I do my best work in environments where architecture, execution, and team effectiveness all need to improve together, not in isolation.

Professional work

Current primary role

Software Engineer Lead · Goodyear

Leading this role today while continuing independent AI and market-analysis exploration outside work.

Software Engineer Lead

Goodyear

March 2022 - Present

January 2024

Continuing as Software Engineer Lead

As Software Engineer Lead, improved scheduler latency from roughly 30 seconds to under one second, supported around 1,000 requests per second, and helped grow the platform from $3M to $15M MRR while reducing rework from 15% to 4.97%.

Featured work

Case studies where technical depth and leadership show up together.

These projects are the clearest examples of architecture, performance, delivery discipline, and business impact reinforcing each other.

Featured

Dynamic scheduling engine

Designed and optimized a slot-based scheduling engine for high-volume booking, rescheduling, and cancellation workflows.

PythonScheduling servicesDatabase optimizationCaching
Featured

AndGo platform scaling and modernization

Led backend and platform modernization across fleet, scheduling, pricing, and engineering systems, pairing architecture direction with measurable improvements in delivery and business outcomes.

PythonFlaskFastAPIGraphQL
Featured

Routing optimization engine

Designed routing optimization for mobile technicians across multi-location field operations, using travel time, service duration, and time windows instead of simplistic distance logic.

Backend servicesOptimization logicTravel-time modelingOperational workflows

Leadership

How I tend to lead when the work gets complex.

The pattern in my work is consistent: solve hard backend and platform problems, then improve the engineering systems around them so the team can keep moving with less friction.

Leadership snapshot

I am usually at my best when architecture, delivery, and team health are all under pressure at the same time. That is where technical judgment and leadership stop being separate skills and start reinforcing each other.

01

I led backend and platform work across an 8-engineer team scope, pairing architecture direction with day-to-day delivery accountability.

02

I introduced stronger design-review rigor, GraphQL and Hasura enablement, onboarding support, and engineering standards that made the team easier to scale.

03

I helped reduce rework from 15% to 4.97% by improving technical quality and execution discipline together instead of treating them as separate problems.

04

My review history consistently points to mentoring, High Potential recognition, and Exceptional Performer ratings.
